name: research-critic
description: Pre-implementation Researcher draft -> Researcher attack workflow for closing task context before downstream planning or implementation. Use when a task needs evidence gathering, assumption pressure, ambiguity cleanup, readiness judgment, or a reusable research packet. Do not use when the task is already approved for direct implementation, needs PR/code review, or should be executed through a larger planning/development workflow.

prompt: Normalize the task input, inspect available evidence, and produce a research packet. In the packet, decide explicitly whether this slice needs a REASONS Canvas. Use yes for fuzzy requirements, AI-assisted non-trivial work, multi-step generation, repeated evolution, cross-role alignment, or meaningful drift risk between intent and code. If yes, also create a separate immutable workflow artifact named `reasons-canvas-research`; fill only the research-owned depth with evidence-backed Requirements, observed domain Entities, candidate options, research-level constraints, risks, norms, safeguards, and explicit open questions or tensions. Do not finalize architecture, file-level operations, or implementation work. If no, state the reason in the research packet. Return ready_for_attack when the packet is ready for researcher attack, needs_input when user answers are required, or blocked when progress is unsafe without external input. If revising after attack feedback or user answers, preserve answered decisions and close only evidence-backed questions.

Loop continuity across workflow loop iterations is prompt based: rely only on explicit input.prompt context and prior step outputs; do not assume persistent draft/attack worker reuse across iterations. If concise clarification is needed, do not ask the user directly; return a clarification request for the orchestrator to relay, then continue in the same clarification session after the orchestrator forwards the user's reply without restart or context widening.
${{ input.research_attack.verdict | default: "No previous research attack verdict." }}

Ask the user only the blocking research questions from the latest available draft-producing output and latest research_attack output. Prefer draft-producing outputs in this deterministic freshness order: research_revision, then research_answered_draft, then research_draft. Continue with approval approved after the user answer is captured in the approval output; use blocked only when the user cannot or will not provide required input.

Revise the research packet using the approved user answers from ask_research_questions and any attack feedback if present. Preserve answered decisions and close only evidence-backed questions. Re-evaluate the REASONS Canvas decision and emit a fresh immutable `reasons-canvas-research` artifact at research-owned depth when required; if not required, keep the explicit reason in the research packet. Return ready_for_attack when the packet is ready for researcher attack, needs_input when more user answers are required, or blocked when progress is unsafe without external input.

Loop continuity across workflow loop iterations is prompt based: rely only on explicit input.prompt context and prior step outputs; do not assume persistent draft/attack worker reuse across iterations. If concise clarification is needed, do not ask the user directly; return a clarification request for the orchestrator to relay, then continue in the same clarification session after the orchestrator forwards the user's reply without restart or context widening.

Start from a hostile prior: assume the change, proposal, draft, or packet is wrong, incomplete, overcomplicated, or under-evidenced until the artifact proves otherwise. Do not give credit for intent, author confidence, green self-reports, or plausible-sounding structure. PASS is allowed only after serious attack finds no evidence-backed blocker or important finding. Do not invent bugs. Any FAIL must be evidence-backed with file/function/line or equivalent precise location, and explain why existing tests/checks did not catch it. Prefer small, evidence-backed blockers over broad commentary.

Attack the latest available research packet for weak evidence, unsafe assumptions, missing user input, misclassified blockers, and over-broad candidate approaches. Also attack the REASONS Canvas decision: if the packet says a Canvas is required, verify the immutable `reasons-canvas-research` artifact exists, captures research-owned Requirements and observed Entities, and leaves architecture/implementation-owned detail as explicit questions, constraints, or tensions rather than fake certainty; if the packet says no Canvas is required, challenge that decision against fuzzy requirements, AI-assistance, non-triviality, drift risk, repeated evolution, and cross-role alignment pressure. Prefer draft-producing outputs in this deterministic freshness order: research_revision, then research_answered_draft, then research_draft. Return approved only when the packet is ready for downstream handoff, needs_revision when a bounded revision should loop through a fresh draft-producing output, needs_input when user answers are required, or blocked for an unsafe stop.

Loop continuity across workflow loop iterations is prompt based: rely only on explicit input.prompt context and prior step outputs; do not assume persistent draft/attack worker reuse across iterations. If concise clarification is needed, do not ask the user directly; return a clarification request for the orchestrator to relay, then continue in the same clarification session after the orchestrator forwards the user's reply without restart or context widening.

Revise the latest available research packet using the latest research_attack verdict and any approved user answers. Prefer draft-producing outputs in this deterministic freshness order: research_revision, then research_answered_draft, then research_draft. Preserve answered decisions, address each bounded attack finding, re-evaluate the REASONS Canvas decision, and emit a fresh immutable `reasons-canvas-research` artifact at research-owned depth when required. Return ready_for_attack when the packet is ready for another researcher attack, needs_input when user answers are required, or blocked when progress is unsafe without external input.

Loop continuity across workflow loop iterations is prompt based: rely only on explicit input.prompt context and prior step outputs; do not assume persistent draft/attack worker reuse across iterations. If concise clarification is needed, do not ask the user directly; return a clarification request for the orchestrator to relay, then continue in the same clarification session after the orchestrator forwards the user's reply without restart or context widening.

Finalize persistence metadata for the approved research packet and any approved `reasons-canvas-research` artifact using the latest approved research packet and latest research_attack verdict. Prefer draft-producing outputs in this deterministic freshness order: research_revision, then research_answered_draft, then research_draft. Return saved after emitting artifact/result references to the saved packet and research Canvas when present, or blocked if the packet cannot be safely saved.

Loop continuity across workflow loop iterations is prompt based: rely only on explicit input.prompt context and prior step outputs; do not assume persistent draft/attack worker reuse across iterations. If concise clarification is needed, do not ask the user directly; return a clarification request for the orchestrator to relay, then continue in the same clarification session after the orchestrator forwards the user's reply without restart or context widening.

input.prompt: Research workflow is blocked. Surface the blocker and required input.
